clinical_detail:
Chief Complaint:
encephalopathy and adrenal mass
 
Major Surgical or Invasive Procedure:
Adrenal biopsy ___

History of Present Illness:
___ with HCV cirrhosis, esophageal varices with history of
bleeding (s/p TIPS on ___, and HCC who presents from liver
tumor clinic today with hepatic encephalopathy and a new adrenal
mass. His medical history is plagued by intermittent
non-adherence and loss of insurance. He had a surveillance CT 
abd
on ___, which revealed a lesion consistent with HCC, as well
as a new, left adrenal mass of unclear etiology. He was seen in
the liver tumor clinic on ___, at which time his 
presentation
was concerning for hepatic encephalopathy, though he reports
adherence to lactulose. He was referred to the hospital for
evaluation of confusion and expedited tumor work-up.

Per patient, he has been feeling increasingly confused and
fatigued for ___ years. He feels he is slow to move around,
endorses DOE. He denies any acute change in his symptoms. He
denies f/c, lightheadedness/dizziness, cough/cold, CP/palp, SOB,
abdominal pain/N/V, dysuria, changes in BM, joint/MSK pain. He
reports a 65lb weight loss that he attributes to intentional
change in his eating habits.  

Of note, his HCV was previous treated with Harvoni, though
therapy with this medication was interrupted by a loss of
insurance. After
[... truncated ...]

Past Medical History:
-bipolar disorder  
-attention deficit disorder  
-Insomnia  
-hepatitis C with cirrhosis  
-tobacco abuse  
-COPD  
-gastroesophageal reflux with ___ esophagus  

 
Social History:
___
Family History:
No known family history of liver disease

Hospital Course:
___ with HCV cirrhosis, esophageal varices with history of 
bleeding (s/p TIPS on ___, and ___ who presents from liver 
tumor clinic with concern for confusion and new adrenal mass.

#ADRENAL MASS: Patient with concerning mass incidentally noted 
on imaging. He endorses significant fatigue and weight loss over 
the course of > ___ year (weight loss seems intentional). He was 
referred into the hospital for expedited evaluation. TSH, 
aldosterone, cortisol, ACTH, renin WNL. Normetanephrines just 
above upper limit of normal at 0.95, free metanephrines normal. 
CT chest w/o metastatic disease and notable for micro nodules, 
bone scan negative for metastatic disease as well. Adrenal 
biopsy occurred ___, and patient will follow up with oncology 
(Dr. ___ and liver doctor (___) on discharge for 
further management. 

#COAG NEGATIVE STAPH BACTEREMIA: Coag negative staph in ___ 
bottles from ___. CXR and UA without evidence of infection. 
No other evidence for nidus of infection, no recent 
instrumentation. No hardware. Recent dental appt but no 
extraction or invasive procedure. In the setting of possible 
decompensation of liver failure with HE (HE was not apparent on 
admission, but was noted in clinic prior to admission), patient 
was covered with IV vanc ___. ID was consulted and found that 
his overall picture was a contaminant and recommended 
discontinuing antibiotic (stopped on ___. Remained HD stable, 
febrile, no leukocytosis, no symptoms. Repeat blood cx negative.

#CON
[... truncated ...]

Discharge Diagnosis (prior stay — from note):
PRIMARY: hepatic encephalopathy, adrenal mass
SECONDARY: HCV/EtOH cirrhosis, hepatocellular carcinoma

clinical_detail:
Chief Complaint:
hepatic encephalopathy, shortness of breath
 
Major Surgical or Invasive Procedure:
none

History of Present Illness:
___ w/ PMH of HCV induced cirrhosis c/b esophageal varices s/p
TIPS on ___ and HCC w/ bx proven mets to left adrenal 
gland;
history ADHD, bipolar disorder, GERD, asthma/COPD, and ___
esophagus presented to OSH with SOB. Patient states that
shortness of breath began yesterday and is not exertional. 
Denies
cough, fever, chest pain, leg swelling, abdominal pain, or
distention. Patient is poor historian. He was transferred here
because he is followed by liver here.

Per review of the ___ note (where he presented on ___,
patient's emergency room course there is summarized as below:
-Presented for evaluation of gradually worsening shortness of
breath over the course of several hours.
-Review of systems positive for epistaxis from the left nare. 
Otherwise notable for no headache, abdominal pain,
nausea/vomiting.
-Lab work notable for mild hyponatremia at 130, troponin of 
0.03,
AST 216, ALT 78, T bili 3.9.
-Patient was given nasal Afrin and thrombin oxygen for his
nosebleed, to improvement of his epistaxis.
-Patient was transferred to ___
per his preference, as he has hepatology following him here.

In the ED, initial VS were:  T 98.1 BP 123/76 HR 98 RR 17 O2 94%
on RA
Exam notable for
[... truncated ...]

Past Medical History:
-bipolar disorder  
-attention deficit disorder  
-Insomnia  
-hepatitis C with cirrhosis  
-tobacco abuse  
-COPD  
-gastroesophageal reflux with ___ esophagus  

 
Social History:
___
Family History:
No known family history of liver disease

Hospital Course:
___ y/o male with a PMHx of HCV (GT3a, treatment failure x2: 
Epclusa+RBV completed ___, previously treated w/Harvoni 
which was interrupted because apparently pt lost insurance) 
induced cirrhosis c/b esophageal varices s/p TIPS on ___ 
and HCC w/ bx proven mets to left adrenal gland; s/p RFA on 
___ to ___ lesion and RFA on ___ to large left 
adrenal met and with a history ADHD, bipolar disorder, GERD, 
asthma/COPD, and ___ esophagus, presenting with shortness 
of breath, found to have altered mental status. 

His shortness of breath he attributed to the hot weather and 
improved once he was in an air-conditioned environment.  He 
never had signs of volume overload His AMS was attributed to 
hepatic encephalopathy in the setting of decreased stooling. He 
reports taking his lactulose at home, but only had 2 BMs/day 
prior to admission instead of his usual 4. In the hospital, his 
lactulose was uptitrated with improvement of his mental status 
back to baseline.

TRANSITIONAL ISSUES:
[ ] GOALS OF CARE DISCUSSION:
- Patient without clear understanding of the next steps of 
therapy for his liver cancer.  Per last clinic note, it had been 
suggested that he meet with Palliative Care as there are no 
further therapies to treat his cancer.  We discussed this fact, 
and the patient was in understanding that there were no 
treatments to offer for his liver cancer.
- Please help the patient schedule a palliative care appointment 
to address his symptomatology, as well as psychosocial
[... truncated ...]

Discharge Diagnosis (prior stay — from note):
Primary diagnosis:
Acute hepatic encephalopathy, improving

Secondary diagnosis:
Hepatitis C cirrhosis
hepatocellular carcinoma
bipolar disorder
Attention deficit hyperactivity disorder
